subject: Public Health
title: Climate Change and Water Security
description: A quick, practical guide to Climate Change and Water Security.

hook.text: Climate change is a water crisis—too much water in floods, too little in droughts, and less predictability.

buildup.text: Rising temperatures melt glaciers that feed rivers, alter rainfall patterns, and raise sea levels into freshwater.

discovery.text: By 2050, up to 5.7 billion people could live in areas with water scarcity for at least one month per year.

twist.text: Water infrastructure designed for past climates is failing—systems must be rebuilt for an uncertain future.

climax.text: Climate-resilient WASH means diversifying water sources, protecting watersheds, and building flexible systems.

punchline.text: The climate crisis is, at its core, a water crisis.

quiz.question: How many people could face water scarcity by 2050?
